title: Data protection: marking 10 years of the GDPR
content: Today marks the 10th anniversary of the General Data Protection Regulation’s (GDPR) adoption, the first data protection framework that established rights for individuals and obligations for organisations across Europe. The European Data Protection Board ensures the GDPR’s enforcement.
snippet: Today marks the 10th anniversary of the General Data Protection Regulation’s (GDPR) adoption, the first data protection framework that established rights for individuals and obligations for organisati

title: 1.4 million people globally suffer acute food insecurity in 2025
content: Some 1.4 million people in Haiti, Mali, Gaza Strip, South Sudan, the Sudan and Yemen faced the most severe level of acute food insecurity in 2025, according to a global report on food crises. The projected outlook for 2026 sees little respite, with continued acute food insecurity in several regions.

title: EU institutions agree roadmap to strengthen Europe’s competitiveness
content: The European Parliament, the Council of the EU and the European Commission have agreed to launch the One Europe, One Market Roadmap that will help advance the EU’s competitiveness agenda. The roadmap sets out concrete actions and targets for agreements, at the latest by end 2027.
snippet: The European Parliament, the Council of the EU and the European Commission have agreed to launch the One Europe, One Market Roadmap that will help advance the EU’s competitiveness agenda. The roadmap

title: Chernobyl 40 years later
content: How we manage nuclear safety and prepare for the future, must be guided by lessons we learn from the past. 40 years since the Chernobyl nuclear disaster, take a look at what the EU is doing to promote nuclear safety around the world and how it has helped to protect Chernobyl from further leakage.

title: European Youth Week 2026
content: European Youth Week 2026 takes place from 24 April to 1 May 2026. It promotes youth engagement, participation, and active citizenship across Europe and beyond. This year’s edition is dedicated to solidarity and fairness as we celebrate 30 years of volunteering in Europe.
snippet: European Youth Week 2026 takes place from 24 April to 1 May 2026. It promotes youth engagement, participation, and active citizenship across Europe and beyond. This year’s edition is dedicated to soli

title: Winners announced of 2026 European Capitals of Inclusion and Diversity Award
content: This year's winners of the 2026 European Capitals of Inclusion and Diversity Award are cities and municipalities from Belgium, Hungary, Italy, the Netherlands, and Spain. They have been recognised for their work to build fairer societies by promoting diversity and inclusion in their neighbourhoods.
snippet: This year's winners of the 2026 European Capitals of Inclusion and Diversity Award are cities and municipalities from Belgium, Hungary, Italy, the Netherlands, and Spain. They have been recognised for

title: Meta found in breach of EU’s Digital Services Act
content: The European Commission has preliminarily found Meta's Instagram and Facebook in breach of the Digital Services Act for failing to reduce the risks of minors under 13 years old accessing their services. The US tech company’s current measures to prevent access do not seem to be effective.
snippet: The European Commission has preliminarily found Meta's Instagram and Facebook in breach of the Digital Services Act for failing to reduce the risks of minors under 13 years old accessing their service

title: New actions to protect Europeans from the fossil fuel crisis
content: In light of the current geopolitical situation, the European Commission has proposed actions to bring immediate energy relief to European households and industries, especially the most vulnerable ones. The actions also create a pathway to energy independence, away from volatile fossil fuel markets.
snippet: In light of the current geopolitical situation, the European Commission has proposed actions to bring immediate energy relief to European households and industries, especially the most vulnerable ones

title: High HPV vaccination coverage can help eliminate cervical cancer
content: As Europe marks European Immunization Week 2026, a new report finds continued progress in HPV vaccination across the EU and European Economic Area. Three countries have reached the EU target of 90% HPV vaccination coverage among girls by the age of 15, reducing the risk of cervical cancer.
snippet: As Europe marks European Immunization Week 2026, a new report finds continued progress in HPV vaccination across the EU and European Economic Area. Three countries have reached the EU target of 90% HP

title: EU has cut its greenhouse gas emissions by 40% since 1990
content: The EU has reduced its greenhouse gas emissions by 40% since 1990, according to new data. The decline in EU net domestic emissions was driven by a larger share of renewable energy, the use of less carbon intensive fossil fuels, improved energy efficiency, and structural economic changes.
snippet: The EU has reduced its greenhouse gas emissions by 40% since 1990, according to new data. The decline in EU net domestic emissions was driven by a larger share of renewable energy, the use of less car

title: EU history: celebrating 75 years since the Treaty of Paris
content: On April 18 we celebrate 75 years since the signing of the Treaty of Paris establishing the European Coal and Steel Community, the Treaty that ultimately led to the creation of the European Union. Take a look at why it came about and what were its intentions.
snippet: On April 18 we celebrate 75 years since the signing of the Treaty of Paris establishing the European Coal and Steel Community, the Treaty that ultimately led to the creation of the European Union. Tak

title: Over 10 million workers trained under the EU’s Pact for Skills
content: Over 10 million workers, including 3.9 million in 2025 alone, have benefited from upskilling initiatives under the EU’s Pact for Skills since its launch in 2020. The pact prioritises training in digital, green, and sector specific skills, reflecting Europe's most pressing labour market needs.
snippet: Over 10 million workers, including 3.9 million in 2025 alone, have benefited from upskilling initiatives under the EU’s Pact for Skills since its launch in 2020. The pact prioritises training in digit

title: European age verification app to keep children safe online
content: The EU has developed a user-friendly age verification app to protect children online. It can be set up using a passport or ID card, allowing users to prove their age when accessing online services. The app is anonymous, works on any device, and is fully open source. It will be available soon.
snippet: The EU has developed a user-friendly age verification app to protect children online. It can be set up using a passport or ID card, allowing users to prove their age when accessing online services. Th

title: New report looks at how algorithms fuel polarisation and affect democracy
content: Overly curated, attention-grabbing content can lead to ‘fractured realities’, a new study warns. These fractured realities can lead to an increase in negative, anti-democratic sentiments across digital information spaces. The study sets out policy recommendations to safeguard the EU’s digital world.
snippet: Overly curated, attention-grabbing content can lead to ‘fractured realities’, a new study warns. These fractured realities can lead to an increase in negative, anti-democratic sentiments across digita

title: EU provides over €800 million in response to crisis in Sudan
content: The EU, together with EU countries, has pledged more than €800 million in aid to Sudan and its neighbouring countries which are currently facing the world's most severe humanitarian emergency. The funds will help with food insecurity, shelter, water, healthcare, protection and education.
snippet: The EU, together with EU countries, has pledged more than €800 million in aid to Sudan and its neighbouring countries which are currently facing the world's most severe humanitarian emergency. The fun

title: ‘Deaf’ wins 2026 LUX Audience Award
content: Chosen by citizens across Europe and Members of the European Parliament, Spanish director Eva Libertad’s film “Deaf” has won the 2026 LUX Audience Award. The aim of the award is to shine a light on experiences beyond our own. Deaf follows the life a deaf woman navigating motherhood.
snippet: Chosen by citizens across Europe and Members of the European Parliament, Spanish director Eva Libertad’s film “Deaf” has won the 2026 LUX Audience Award. The aim of the award is to shine a light on ex

title: Too few urban green spaces in European cities, study finds
content: A study of more than 800 European cities reveals the need to bring nature back into cities. It also identifies a "green divide" where wealthier neighbourhoods enjoy far higher levels of tree cover and proximity to nature than lower-income areas. Climate also impacts access to urban green spaces.
snippet: A study of more than 800 European cities reveals the need to bring nature back into cities. It also identifies a "green divide" where wealthier neighbourhoods enjoy far higher levels of tree cover and

title: Entry/Exit System for non-EU nationals becomes fully operational
content: Starting today, the EU’s new border management system is officially fully operational across all Schengen countries. The automated system replaces the old way of manually stamping passports. Registering non-EU nationals travelling to the EU now becomes more secure, efficient and convenient.
snippet: Starting today, the EU’s new border management system is officially fully operational across all Schengen countries. The automated system replaces the old way of manually stamping passports. Registeri

title: EU invests close to €400 million for postdoctoral researchers
content: Are you a researcher? Apply now for a new round of the EU's Marie Skłodowska-Curie Actions Postdoctoral Fellowships, worth almost €400 million. This programme supports PhD researchers to carry out research activities abroad and acquire new skills in different disciplines.
snippet: Are you a researcher? Apply now for a new round of the EU's Marie Skłodowska-Curie Actions Postdoctoral Fellowships, worth almost €400 million. This programme supports PhD researchers to carry out res

title: Turning 18? Apply for a free DiscoverEU travel pass
content: The EU is offering DiscoverEU travel passes to 18-year-olds to explore Europe by train. Applications are open until 22 April 2026. You can travel anytime between 1 July 2026 and 30 September 2027 for up to 7 days within a one-month period.
snippet: The EU is offering DiscoverEU travel passes to 18-year-olds to explore Europe by train. Applications are open until 22 April 2026. You can travel anytime between 1 July 2026 and 30 September 2027 for

title: 2025 was EU’s most destructive wildfire season on record
content: The 2025 fire season was the worst on record for the European Union. Satellite-based analysis from the European Commission’s Joint Research Centre show that more than 1 million hectares – equivalent to approximately the area of Cyprus – were ravaged by fire across several EU countries.
snippet: The 2025 fire season was the worst on record for the European Union. Satellite-based analysis from the European Commission’s Joint Research Centre show that more than 1 million hectares – equivalent t

title: New rules to address bank failures protecting taxpayers and depositors
content: New rules will help better protect taxpayers and depositors by broadening the scope of banks covered by the EU’s laws on bank failure. They also empower authorities to manage potential bank failures more effectively and harmonise depositor protection across the EU.
snippet: New rules will help better protect taxpayers and depositors by broadening the scope of banks covered by the EU’s laws on bank failure. They also empower authorities to manage potential bank failures m

title: EU check reveals misleading sales practices online
content: The European Commission and consumer protection authorities have released the results of a screening of online discounts during Black Friday and Cyber Monday sales. Some discounts were misleading and did not comply with EU consumer law.
snippet: The European Commission and consumer protection authorities have released the results of a screening of online discounts during Black Friday and Cyber Monday sales. Some discounts were misleading and

title: Reform to the EU customs union agreed
content: Parliament and Council have agreed on the most ambitious reform of EU customs rules since 1968. The new rules introduce new measures for e-commerce and modern, data-driven customs architecture that simplifies procedures and enhances efficiency.
snippet: Parliament and Council have agreed on the most ambitious reform of EU customs rules since 1968. The new rules introduce new measures for e-commerce and modern, data-driven customs architecture that si

title: EU to modernise how it tackles rising wildfire threat
content: The Commission has put forward a new strategy for wildfire risk management that covers prevention, preparedness, response and recovery. 2025 was the worst wildfire season in Europe since records began. This strategy will help enhance resilience to wildfires and protect people and the environment.
snippet: The Commission has put forward a new strategy for wildfire risk management that covers prevention, preparedness, response and recovery. 2025 was the worst wildfire season in Europe since records began

title: EU road deaths drop by 3% in 2025
content: New figures show 3% fewer people died on European roads in 2025 compared to 2024. Some 19,400 road fatalities were reported in 2025, 580 fewer than the previous year. However, most EU countries are not yet on track to meet the EU's goal of halving road deaths and serious injuries by 2030.
snippet: New figures show 3% fewer people died on European roads in 2025 compared to 2024. Some 19,400 road fatalities were reported in 2025, 580 fewer than the previous year. However, most EU countries are no

title: EU and Australia strengthen ties with trade agreement
content: The EU and Australia have concluded negotiations on a free trade agreement. It will remove over 99% of tariffs on EU exports to Australia, improve access to critical raw materials, and strengthen strategic ties with the Indo-Pacific. They also signed a Security and Defence Partnership.
snippet: The EU and Australia have concluded negotiations on a free trade agreement. It will remove over 99% of tariffs on EU exports to Australia, improve access to critical raw materials, and strengthen stra

title: EU grants €2.7 billion to clean industry projects
content: Some 54 clean industry projects will receive €2.7 billion of funding from the EU’s Innovation Fund. The projects span 17 countries and will support innovative, decarbonisation solutions such as refineries, cement and lime, renewable energy, as well as maritime, aviation and road transport.
snippet: Some 54 clean industry projects will receive €2.7 billion of funding from the EU’s Innovation Fund. The projects span 17 countries and will support innovative, decarbonisation solutions such as refine

title: EU funds literary projects for translation and publication
content: Creative Europe will fund the translation of nearly 500 works of fiction from 36 into 24 languages. In the next three years, the works, mostly youth literature and novels, will be made available to readers across Europe and beyond, showcasing the rich linguistic diversity of European literature.
snippet: Creative Europe will fund the translation of nearly 500 works of fiction from 36 into 24 languages. In the next three years, the works, mostly youth literature and novels, will be made available to re

title: Remembering the Brussels terror attacks 10 years later
content: This week marks 10 years since the terrorist attacks in Brussels in 2016. As commemorations take place to remember the victims and survivors, we take look at what the EU is doing to fight terrorism and extremism and to boost security.
